item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In my headcanon, D-class sources are much more varied than death rows, but it looks like most people are like, "if we don't bring up Protocol 12, it's a death row inmate. So it's always interesting to see something like a D-class that had been blind since she 2.

What did you do?!

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This is an anomaly that heavily relates to dream based anomalies, the ending of which implies the existence of several dream-based entities. As such, I feel that linking to the greater site canon about dream entities would be appropriate. My first thought would be MTF Omicron-Rho.

Editing this:

D-1394-5 remains in a fetal position; attempts to alter her posture, including those involving heavy application of force, have failed. D-1394-5 responds to visual stimuli, but exhibits no signs of consciousness. Samples indicate that D-1394-5's blood contains 12% black sand by volume.

To this:

D-1394-5 remains in a fetal position; attempts to alter her posture, including those involving heavy application of force, have failed. D-1394-5 responds to visual stimuli, but exhibits no signs of consciousness. Samples indicate that D-1394-5's blood contains 12% black sand by volume. A further attempt at communication by MTF Omicron Rho with the entities encountered has been proposed.

If anyone has any other ideas, please feel free to suggest them.

Time expired, edits made as discussed above. Author may revert at any time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's something that remained ununderstood, at least for me. So Doctor Braun couldn't use the syringe anymore because D-1394-5 still had it in her dream?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I remember the anomalous murder doctor this once was, if this is the right number.
Much more interesting now, nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was gonna be like, "eh, dream skip, no", but then the experiments were interesting, but then the last one was like "dream skiiiiip", but then the final interview was really interesting. So I think you have a winner, all told.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That presumption is correct. I've fixed that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Quite good, just one thing though

Samples indicate that SCP-1394's blood contains 12% black sand by volume.

I presume you meant D-1394-5?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure what's going on with the black sand

My guess?

Exit light...

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the angle of people thinking they've fixed something. That really works for me, and it's just weird in general. Especially the dude's non-existent tooth still working. That shit is always wild. I'm not sure what's going on with the black sand or that last interview, but I'm digging it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that's all it needed. Concise is nicer, especially at that point where you're just setting up the premise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I changed it to this:

When SCP-1394 is used […] the subject will begin to sleepwalk four minutes after their next onset of REM sleep.

The two suggestions you gave felt too… bulky, so I tried to work out something that's less ambiguous without being too wordy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A suggestion
– hide block

This did some confuse.

When SCP-1394 is used […] the subject will begin to sleepwalk four minutes after falling into REM sleep.

Does the subject asleep after the injection, or does the sleepwalking happen next time the subject naturally falls asleep? Maybe rewrite like:

When SCP-1394 is used […] the subject will immediately fall into REM sleep, and begin sleep walking four minutes later.

or else

When SCP-1394 is used […] there is no immediate effect, but the subject will begin to sleepwalk four minutes after the next onset of REM sleep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are the interviews with D-1394-5 being conducted with onieronautics, or is she somambulant or what?

item.title: a man bursts into a hospital, worried. he talks to the receptionist, who points him to his right. he speaks to a doctor.
item.content:encoded: Hey hey hey this is a rewrite of the original SCP-1394, by MookVanguard. The image is a modified version of this image by Wellcome Images, which is under a CC BY 4.0 license.
